channel.title: Two Grifters
channel.description: Two friends walk through history's greatest heists, cons, and scams: who planned them, how they worked, and the one dumb detail that brought them down. Hosted by Frankie and Ruby. Every episode is built from the historical record, and the sources are in the show notes.
channel.itunes:author: Two Grifters

item.title: The Antwerp Diamond Heist: Beaten by a Sandwich
item.description:
In February 2003, a crew of Italian thieves emptied 109 safe deposit boxes beneath the most secure building in the world's diamond capital, without tripping a single alarm. They beat a three-ton vault door, seismic sensors, and a lock with one hundred million combinations. Then a half-eaten salami sandwich brought the whole thing down.
Sources: - Joshua Davis, 'The Untold Story of the World's Biggest Diamond Heist,' Wired, March 2009: https://www.wired.com/2009/03/ff-diamonds-2/ - Scott Andrew Selby and Greg Campbell, 'Flawless: Inside the Largest Diamond Heist in History,' Union Square Press, 2010 - BBC News coverage of the February 2003 Antwerp Diamond Centre theft and the 2005 trial of Leonardo Notarbartolo

item.title: Pipeline Test: The Sandwich
item.description: A six-turn mini episode used to verify the Two Grifters production pipeline end to end. Not for publication.
